title: Smoke-clearing room spray
subtitle: A citrus and juniper room spray for neutralising cooking smells and stale air
excerpt: This room spray uses citrus essential oils and juniper berry to cut through cooking smells, stale air, and lingering odours. Bicarbonate of soda in the formula absorbs acidic odour molecules rather than simply masking them. Spray after cooking, before guests arrive, or whenever a room smells stale. Shake well before each use.
sourceNotes: Sodium bicarbonate as an odour absorber: reacts with acidic odour compounds (volatile fatty acids, acetic acid from cooking) to form odourless salts. Citrus essential oils (limonene) as top notes in air fresheners: IFRA standard for room sprays. Juniper berry EO (Juniperus communis): piney, fresh character; IFRA standard for room sprays. Witch hazel as dispersant: see eucalyptus-mint-room-spray source notes. The bicarbonate solution should be mixed with care to avoid fizzing excessively.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable for several months. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps 6 months in a sealed spray bottle in a cool dark place. The bicarbonate may settle; shake before each use. Discard if the spray becomes oily or the fragrance changes.

glossaryTerms.definition: A substance that chemically reacts with and neutralises odour molecules rather than masking them with a stronger fragrance. Bicarbonate of soda neutralises acidic cooking odours by forming odourless salts.

content.text: Most air fresheners mask smells with a stronger fragrance. This one takes a different approach: bicarbonate of soda dissolved in the formula acts as an
for acidic cooking smells. The citrus and juniper oils give a clean, fresh finish that clears quickly. Use in kitchens, hallways, and living rooms.

items.prepNote: half a teaspoon; dissolved in the water

content.text: Dissolve the bicarbonate of soda in the distilled water first and stir until fully dissolved. Add the essential oils to the witch hazel and swirl to combine. Pour both liquids into the spray bottle. Seal and shake well. The mixture may appear slightly hazy.

Shake before each use. Spray into the centre of the room or towards soft furnishings. For a kitchen after cooking, spray into the air and towards curtains or soft seating that may have absorbed the smell. Ventilate the room at the same time if possible.
Keep in a cool dark place. Keeps 6 months. The bicarbonate will settle; shake before each use. Discard if the fragrance fades significantly or the spray becomes oily.
